questions.text: What was your favourite holiday, festival or family celebration when you were growing up?
Describe the decorations, lights, smells or sounds that filled your home during your favourite holiday.
What special food, dish or treat did you only get during holidays or big family gatherings?
Did your family have any quirky, funny or unique traditions that other people found strange?
What was your favourite gift-giving or gift-receiving moment from childhood holidays?
Was there a song, movie, story, game or ritual that was always part of a certain celebration?
Who usually led or organised the holiday preparations in your family — how did they do it?
Did you have any traditions that were just for your immediate family or close relatives?
Was there a holiday tradition you looked forward to all year long?
Did you ever help create or start a new tradition with friends or your own family later on?
Was there a holiday or celebration that felt different, difficult or bittersweet one year?
Looking back, which tradition taught you the most about your family’s values or history?
Is there a tradition from childhood that you still carry on today — or one you wish you could bring back?
What one holiday memory makes you feel most connected to your family or younger self?
If you could design one perfect holiday tradition for future generations of your family, what would it include?
Was there a holiday tradition that quietly shaped how you think about belonging or family?
What do you hope your children or grandchildren experience in the way of celebrations or traditions?
Is there a holiday or celebration memory that still brings up strong feelings today?
Did any holiday tradition ever cause tension, disappointment or conflict in your family?
Looking back, what one tradition do you now realise was a way your family showed love or resilience?
What part of your childhood celebrations do you consciously want to recreate or avoid in your own life?
If you could go back and relive one holiday exactly as it was, which one would it be and why?
What do you hope people who shared those traditions with you remember about those times?
Is there a holiday tradition or celebration that you now see as a quiet turning point in your family story?
What message about family, joy or togetherness do you hope your holiday memories pass on?
If your younger self could see how you celebrate (or don’t celebrate) today, what do you think they would feel?
